error.signature: AI tells a visitor to South Africa to plan around 'occasional power outages' without explaining stage-based load shedding schedules and indoor/security risks

verdict.summary: South Africa's Eskom-managed grid has periodic 'load shedding' — scheduled rolling blackouts to prevent national grid collapse. Stages range 1-8; stage 6+ means most areas without power 8-12 hours/day in 2-4 hour blocks. Schedules per municipality, published via Eskom SE Push app. Affects: traffic lights (intersections become 4-way stops), ATMs, fuel pumps, hotel lifts, internet, mobile data (some), security alarms. Plan around it.

dead_ends.action: Tell a visitor to plan dinner reservations at 19:00 in Johannesburg without checking load-shedding schedule
dead_ends.why_fails: Load shedding can hit any 2-4 hour window. Many small restaurants close during blackouts; hotels with generators stay open. Without checking the day's schedule, dinner plans collapse routinely. EskomSePush app is the de-facto schedule lookup.

dead_ends.action: Drive through Joburg / Cape Town intersections during load shedding without preparing for traffic-light failures
dead_ends.why_fails: When power's out, traffic lights default to off — every intersection becomes 4-way stop convention. Locals know this; tourists often don't. Accident risk spikes. SAPS warns to treat dark intersections as 4-way stops.

dead_ends.action: Use ATMs, gas stations, or rely on home alarms during stage 4+ load shedding
dead_ends.why_fails: Many ATMs, gas pumps, and security systems lose power. Home alarms beep during outage and may trigger false alarms (or miss real ones during gap). Stock cash before outages.

workarounds.action: Install EskomSePush app — provides schedule by area (need municipality + suburb), real-time stage notifications, and outage logs. Free tier sufficient for most.
Stay at hotels with backup generators (most 4-5 star hotels). Confirm at booking. Some malls run on generator continuously. Boutique B&Bs may not have backup.
Driving: treat dark intersections as 4-way stops; alternate yielding. Watch for guards manually directing at major junctions. Allow 2x travel time during stage 4+.
Carry: power bank charged before outage, small flashlight, cash for shops without working POS. Mobile data often degrades during outages — download offline maps.
